rulings.Case Title: C. OVERAA & CO., INC. VS. U.S. GLASS & ALUMINUM, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Compelling Defts' Further Responses To Pltf'S Special Interrogatories (Set One) And Requests For Identification And Production Of Documents (Set One)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 18, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF C. OVERAA & CO., INC. Notice Of Motion Compelling Defts' Further Responses To Pltf'S Special Interrogatories (Set One) And Requests For Identification And Production Of Documents (Set One) Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: HAIG A. HARRIS JR. VS. AMBER A. HAASE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 18, 2012, Line 20, CROSS DEFENDANTS HAIG HARRIS, NEIL TURNER, SCAMPINI, MORTARA & HARRIS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion on complaint: Denied. Nothing in the papers informs the court on the nature of the cause of action sued on or the subject of the motion. The separate statement of undisputed facts does not comport with CRC 3.1350 (d)(separately identify each of action, claim, defense, etc. at issue). Accordingly it is not possible to know if moving party is entitled to judgment as a matter of law. Cf., Kojababian v. Genuine Home Loans, Inc., 174 Cal. App. 4th 408, 416 (2009)(moving party requirement). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: PETER CHIU VS. MAJED FAKHOURI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reconsider Denial Of Motion To Vacate Clerk'S Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 18, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT MAJED FAKHOURI'S Motion To Reconsider Denial Of Motion To Vacate Clerk'S Entry Of Judgment. Denied. At the July 31, 2012 hearing on the motion to vacate, Defendant failed to present a satisfactory reason for its delay in filing the motion to vacate. At that hearing, Defendant failed to raise the issues regarding defense counsel's divorce and residential move. Defendant now fails to provide a valid reason for not offering those facts earlier. As Plaintiff's counsel aptly phrases the situation in his Reply, "tardy facts are not new facts." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: PACIFIC MERCHANT SHIPPING ASSOCIATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motionfo Rissuance Of A Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 18, 2012, Line 4, PETITIONER PACIFIC MERCHANT SHIPPING ASSOCIATION'S Motion for Rissuance Of A Writ Of Mandate. Granted. The Port Agent is a public official; among other things, the position was created by the Legislature. The "Pilot Logs" are documents used by the Port Agent in the execution of his duties including, but not limited to, assigning pilots to vessels and preparing and administering pilot vacation time. These are necessary and convenient to the Port Agent's public duties and are public documents. There is no showing that these documents contain propriety, trade secret, or privileged material. Within thirty days of today's date [the date of the hearing], the Port Agent must produce the requested "Pilot Logs" from 2002, 2003, 2004, 2005, 2006, 2007, 2008, 2009, 2010, and 2011. A detailed description a "Pilot Log" is found in Exhibit W attached to the Petition, described at 15 of the Petition, and exemplified by Petition Exs. H, I, and J. The Court declines to direct Respondents to create documents for purposes of disclosure here, declaratory relief is not useful, and the Petition is otherwise denied. The request for judicial notice is granted. The parties may advise if they require a formal issuance of a writ. Any motion for fees may be made separately, and must be noticed before Judge Karnow in Dept. 608 (after conferring with opposing counsel and Judge Karnow's clerk to arrange a suitable date). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: ENVIRONMENTAL RESEARCH CENTER, VS. AMS HEALTH SCIENCES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Approve Settlement And For Entry Judgment; P & A
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 18, 2012, Line 6, PLAINTIFF ENVIRONMENTAL RESEARCH CENTER'S Motion To Approve Settlement And For Entry Judgment. Denied without prejudice. The papers are not clear on these issues. (1) regarding the insignificant civil penalty, what in fact was the volume of Covered Products sold in this state? I.e. what does "very small" and "miniscule" mean in term of volume of each product? (2) Why should attorney Freund be paid anything for his time spent on 'programmatic efforts' (and what does that mean?); (3) regarding the proposed consent judgment: under section 3.5 should not the test results be promptly sent to plaintiffs' counsel? (4) it is not clear that the extent of the release under section 8.1 correlates with the failure to impose any obligation on defendant for post September 2011 dales (see section 3.6), i.e. counsel are invited to explain whether defendant secures release for post September 2011 sales for which it incurs no obligation under the settlement. These issues may be addressed in supplemental papers to be filed in contemplation of a further hearing before Judge Karnow to be set after consultation with counsel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: PLACE2PARK LLC, et al VS. CITY AND C0UNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A CHARTERED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 18, 2012, Line 17, Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction. Denied. The City is obliged to provide post-payment judicial review of taxes imposed; it is not required to provide a pre-payment method of review, either judicial or administrative. Post payment judicial review is available. The present motion seeks to avoid the 'pay first, litigate later' rule. See, Flying Dutchaman Park, Inc. v. City and County of San Francisco, 93 Ca.App.4th 1129 (2001); Batt v. City and County of San Francisco, 155 Cal.App.4th 65 (2007). Petitioners thus have a low likelihood of success on the merits. The City would be seriously injured if were unable to collect the taxes at issue while the balance of litigation ensued. On balance, the factors governing the issuance of preliminary injunctive relief favor denial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)
